title: SUUNTO 7 GPS Sports Smart Watch
features: Suunto 7: This versatile smart watch combines Suunto's sports expertise with smartwatch technology; Track exercise accurately with access to more than 70 sports modes, GPS, and wrist heart rate sensor

Make Life Easier: Compatible with Android and iOS phones, the Suunto 7 is powered by Wear OS by Google so you can see incoming calls and messages, make payments with your watch and download music to your watch to listen offline
Get Connected: Connect to your favorite sports apps and services from the Suunto app to get more from your watch; Share with your sports communities and access specific training analysis and guidance
Adventure Starts Here: Combining Scandinavian design with ultra-durable materials, our watches track your sports, daily activity, and sleep to help you keep life, training and recovery in balance
Authentic Heritage: Founded in 1936, Suunto brings over 80 years of high-quality heritage craftsmanship, relentless accuracy, and pioneering innovation to our watches, compasses, and dive products

reviews.title: Waste of money
reviews.text: Don’t waste your money

reviews.title: I like this one better than my other smart watch
reviews.text: I have a big deal designer's name brand smart watch which has a battery life of like 12 hours max and that's with disabling just about everything!  The problem with charging that (and this) smart watch is the fact that you can't just plug them in anywhere with a regular micro usb.  They have their proprietary charging "cradle" that gloms onto the back of the watch.  This one seem so far to have a higher battery life without having to shut down every app to extend the battery life.  The only thing I would wish is that if they market these to both men and women, MAKE THE WATCH FACE A BIT SMALLER!!!  I understand about seeing/reading everything on the watch, but JEEZ it is HUGE!!!  I had to fasten it further down on my wrist/arm so the watch face fit my arm.  I'm not a tiny woman nor am I a big one, so if that's a problem for an average woman, please take note and scale it back by about 3%.  Otherwise, this is a great smart watch.  Runs on Google OS which my other watch runs on and seems to do pretty much the same things.

reviews.title: My husband LOVES this watch!
reviews.text:
I got this watch for my husband who is 6'3. This watch fits his wrist really well. He has used this for over a month before the review.

CONS (very minor):
1. If you move your hand the right way it can hit the buttons on the side and start apps by accident
2. It can be sensitive when moving your hand around and turn on the clock display/run down the battery faster
3. I don't like that the charger is so specific. It would be nice to be able to have more than 1 option to charge

PROS:
1. Battery Life: Easily lasts 1.5 days before charging and it was fine. If it runs down really low it will automatically go into battery saving mode and only functions as a normal watch. It charges very quickly
2. Style: It looks nice. The face size is a good size and easy to read. The band is silicone, comfortable and holds up well to a lot of use. My husband has received many compliments on it. You can change the clock face to a few different options and customize it to you.
3. Alerts: When you get notifications of a message or call the watch vibrates. The watch has a microphone but no speakers so you cannot have a phone call through it or play music. You can type a message or even do a voice to text using the watch.
4. Calendar: The calendar function is nice and gives notifications for upcoming events. It makes it nice when he is in a meeting especially
5. Exercise: It took a while to figure out, but the exercise tracking is nice. My husband doesn't run but does like to lift weights and do other exercise. He was even able to calibrate the watch and it will count his reps for him. It also tracks heart rate, which you can set to track throughout the day. It also tracks steps.
6. GPS: The watch has a GPS and map function. You can see where you are and toggle the maps around to see routes around you. You can also set up multiple route paths ahead if you run/bike etc.
7. Durability: My husband swam with it in a saltwater pool and had no issues, it is definitely water safe. He has hit it accidentally on a few corners/desks and the glass has held up really well, no scratches. The touch sensitivity is great, easy to control but not OVERLY sensitive.

Overall it is a great watch!

reviews.title: Luxury, time and tracking for the seasoned athlete or outdoorsman!
reviews.text:
First things first: you have to be a true athlete or outdoorsman to really want to spend this kind of money on a smartwatch when there are others, but the features on this one make it exceptional. My son has truly been able to maintain his sanity after having to leave school and fly solo during this crisis. He has thrown himself into a variety of workouts to keep himself busy since he can't socialize and thinks this watch is one of the best items he owns.

The GPS features allow him to explore routes, trails, and areas he wasn't previously acquainted with (offline too!) and the health data points are accurate. There are dozens of sports modes for whatever activity he chooses for the day and the display itself is beautiful, bright and very responsive.

I wouldn't pay this much for a watch because I prefer a sedentary lifestyle (LOL) but for the athlete in your family with a long and hot summer ahead, this is the perfect graduation gift too!

Absolutely amazing watch. I have and iPhone and works flawlessly. Only inconvenience is the battery, it barely make it thru the day even don't using gps or other sporty functions. It would be perfect if only
Suunto fix that, otherwise is better than the Ultra for me.

reviews.title: Amazing Feature Packed Smart Watch at a Spectacular Cost
reviews.text:
This VALYV K22 Military Sport Watch is packed with an amazing number of smart features and provided seamless integration with my Samsung smart phone. The display is crystal-clear, and the audio capability and volume are very impressive. In general, it’s a very handsome watch and has a very intuitive user interface. The price is unbelievably low in comparison to some of the other smart watches on the market.
I especially enjoy the built-in fitness, activity, and sports tracking features which have already proven to be invaluable while training at the gym as well as general health monitoring during my activity throughout the day.
This watch charges quickly using the included USB magnetically connected cable and the battery is long lasting once charged. The user interface is customizable through the app – I preferred the default one that it shipped with… but there are MANY other options to choose from.
I summary, after spending many years not even wearing a watch… I am very happy with this purchase and can definitely recommend the purchase of this useful product to others.

reviews.title: Really great for the price!
reviews.text: I don’t spend a lot of money on things like this so I didn’t know what to expect since it’s not $300+… it’s not bad for the price! It works well, the stats seem fairly accurate, except the blood pressure which is always showing ridiculously good (I wish)!! I have high blood pressure and this watch has me at 117/ 68 most of the time. Other then that the only problem I have is the charge! It has a cable with 2 tiny magnets, you have to line them up just so and as soon as you’ve got it, it pops off!!

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: This watch did everything I needed it to do.  It keeps very accurate time and has many excellent features.  The ones I was most interested in were the sleep tracker, heart and blood pressure monitor and step counter as these  are medically necessary.  The accuracy was top of the line.  My only issue is this watch caused the area on my skin that contacted the watch to get a bad rash.  While the rash was essentially harmless, it is caused by a chemical reaction in my body.  Not the watches fault, and I've experienced this side effect with other watches in the past.  Now I use it only occasionally and for only a couple of days at a time to still track the previously mentioned condition.  It's a great watch that I would recommend.

reviews.title: Great Apple alternative!
reviews.text: Got this for my mom, here it is next to my Apple Watch, it has pretty much all the same features as the Apple at about a quarter of the price. The battery lasts all day and seems to be on par with the battery life of my watch. The app is easy to use and my mom loves it!

reviews.title: Awesome Smart Watch
reviews.text:
For the money, this is an awesome smart watch that has some of the same popular features as that “fruit” brand.
The watch was packaged securely in a box and included a User Manual and a “How to Connect with the smart watch” brochure.

My watch was received with 46% battery life and did not need to be charged immediately, but I noticed that the charge cord is magnetic and attaches easily to the back of the watch.

I read the instructions and powered on the watch. I downloaded the app and proceeded to pair, referred to as “bind” in the manual. The smart watch paired effortlessly with my iPhone 13. Other than pairing or unpairing, the user manual doesn’t cover much else, but I found the watch use to be intuitive. I do think a more detailed user manual would be helpful to explain navigating and swipe direction of the screens.

The watch has many functions and I was pleasantly surprised that the watch provided a “Siri connection” and performed flawlessly. I compared the blood pressure and oxygen sensors to my individual monitors to check accuracy. Surprisingly, the watch is pretty accurate.

As I mentioned earlier, I started using the watch with 46% battery life and wore the watch for approximately 25 hours before I received a low battery alert.

This is a great buy and so much so, that within hours of using my watch, I ordered another one for my husband.

reviews.title: Best watch yet!
reviews.text: I'm very impressed with this smart watch. It has so many features and works well. Definitely recommend it.

reviews.title: Great watch, much better than my old "smart" watch
reviews.text:
I got Moto 360 several years ago when smart watches first came out and it was nice, but annoying. It was tied into everything and kept giving me alerts and so eventually I got tired of it. My wife also had a first generation Samsung Gear watch and that was about the same as the Moto.
Well, fast forward a few years and now my wife, daughter, son and daughter-in-law all have newer smart watches (Apple Watches and Fitbit) and they all love them. It was time to get a new phone, so I got a Samsung S20 FE and then decided to get the Samsung watch. I saw on the reviews that it really works well with Samsung phones and so I decided to get the Watch 3. I was looking at the Watch 2, which had a few less features and then I found the Watch 3 in the 41mm size and then found a refurbished one so I ended up paying about the same for the Watch 3 as I would have for the Watch 2.
I'm very impressed with the watch and really love it. I didn't get the one with a cell phone, it just connects to my phone with bluetooth and/or wifi, but it's really amazing. I get my messages, and can turn off the ones that I don't want and then I also get all the health apps. So, now it tells me to get off my chair and walk around and does EKG and stress tesing and Oxygen levels..
I was concerned that the 41 mm might be too small, but it is a great size for me as you can see from the pic. I have had a Citizen Echo Drive for more than 10 years and it is a little larger, but I like the size of the Watch 3 better.
If you are thinking about getting a watch, this one is a good deal right now and I believe it does work best if you use it with a Samsung phone.

reviews.title: Love the watch, hate the battery.
reviews.text: I don't use the sleep tracking because the watch is bulky and annoying while trying to sleep. The mobile app is ok, constantly running in background.  The battery life sucks, only good for a day and sometimes a day and a half which is to bad because I do love all the things it does. I think I can answer my phone on my watch (still figuring out how) can definitely hang up on unwanted calls and can read messages.  Good pedometer and workout tool. Overall nice watch with crappy battery life.

reviews.title: I like it.

title: Garmin Forerunner® 955 Solar, GPS Running Smartwatch with Solar Charging Capabilities, Tailored to Triathletes, Long-Lasting Battery, Whitestone
description: Lead the pack with Forerunner® 955 Solar, the premium GPS running and triathlon smartwatch with Power Glass™ solar charging to extend battery life. The training readiness feature tells you when you’re primed for a productive session — while daily suggested workouts adapt to your performance and recovery alongside the race widget, which provides training tips, course details and completion time predictions. With morning report, you can get an overview of your sleep, recovery, training and more — as well as a picture of your overall health with heart rate variability (HRV) status (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics). On the run, new multi-band GPS and full-color, built-in maps help you navigate confidently. And with traditional buttons and a touch display, it has never been easier to tap into your training status and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained. If you’re pushing toward the podium — or to the very limits — Forerunner 955 Solar is by your side with up to 20 days of battery life with solar charging in smartwatch mode (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions).
features: Built with an always-on, full-color display that’s light on the wrist and easy to read even in direct sunlight

Battery life: up to 20 days of battery life in smartwatch mode for a full picture of your health — from sleep to training (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 42 hours in GPS mode and up to 49 hours with solar charging (assuming continuous use for entire period in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 110 hours in UltraTrac mode with solar charging (assuming use in 50,000 lux conditions)
Click or swipe through maps and stats with traditional button controls or a new, responsive touchscreen
Whether you’re running on city streets or riding densely covered trails, Forerunner 955 provides full-color, built-in mapping to keep you on track

Access multiple global navigation satellite systems, and get access to multiple frequencies sent by navigation satellites for improved accuracy in areas where GNSS signals are reflected, weak or typically don't penetrate — giving you superior accuracy for how far and how fast you’ve run — even in challenging environments
From the moment you wake up, get a training readiness score based on your sleep quality, recovery, training and more, so you can determine whether it’s a good day to go hard — or take it easy
Get ready for your next race with race widget — which provides training tips, personalized daily suggested workouts and completion time predictions based on course details, weather and performance

Using new indicators such as HRV status, your recent exercise history and performance, get an easy insight into your overall effort with training status — and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained

Receive an overview of your sleep, recovery and training outlook as soon as you wake up with morning report — alongside HRV status, training readiness and weather; you can even customize your report to show what you want to see (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

With HRV status, gain a deeper understanding of your overall health, recovery and training performance through heart rate variability while you sleep, based on technology developed by our Firstbeat Analytics team (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

reviews.title: A great watch for a runner
reviews.text: Haven't found anything I don't like about this watch.  Have begun following it's training advice, and it appears to be working.  Provides a wealth of interesting information.  One real plus for us old guys: the face is larger than the 735XT making it easier for these old eyes to read it.

reviews.title: Buena compra
reviews.text: Llego excelente, bien empacado

reviews.title: Resumen de datos y métricas de seguimiento
reviews.text: Verdaderamente útil, el seguimiento de sueño, entreno, potencia, y muchos mas datos que nos permiten dar un seguimiento más preciso del estado físico.

reviews.title: VO2 and race prediction are garbage
reviews.text:
For some reason with the 955 - Garmin decided to stop updating VO2 and race prediction - so if you have come to rely/look at that - don't get this watch. They have tried fixing it with the last few beta updates, but they can't seem to figure out how to correct it.

Multi-band GPS is nice - which honestly is the only reason we all haven't moved back to the 945/735 - which are AMAZING watches.

reviews.title: Fantastic update
reviews.text:
The 955 is a fantastic update over the previous 9XX, 7XX, and 6XX models. This update I'd much more communicative and provides loads more encouraging updates and workout suggestions. The interface is also more fluid than previous models. The return of touch to the user interface is nice but, optional.

Definitely worth going with

EASILY CUSTOMIZABLE — Available in a range of sizes and colors, with dozens of bands to choose from and watch faces with complications tailored to whatever you’re into.

HEALTH AND SAFETY FEATURES — Get help when you need it with Crash Detection, Fall Detection, and Emergency SOS. Get deep insights into your health, including notifications if you have an irregular rhythm or an unusually high or low heart rate.

SIMPLY COMPATIBLE — It works seamlessly with your Apple devices and services. Unlock your Mac automatically. Find your devices with a tap. Pay and send money with Apple Pay. Apple Watch requires an iPhone 8 or later with the latest iOS version.
SWIMPROOF AND STYLISH — Water resistant to 50 meters. Three finishes. And a redesigned, color-matched back case made with a new production process that reduces its carbon emissions over 80 percent.
APPS ON YOUR WRIST — With access to tens of thousands of apps on the App Store right on your wrist, Apple Watch is the perfect personal device for whatever you love to do.

A POWERFUL FITNESS PARTNER — The enhanced Workout app gives you new ways to train and advanced metrics for more information about your workout performance. And Apple Watch comes with three months of Apple Fitness+ free.
STAY CONNECTED — Send a text, make a call, listen to music and podcasts, use Siri, or call for help with Emergency SOS. Apple Watch SE (GPS) works with your iPhone or Wi-Fi to keep you connected.

APPLECARE — Every Apple Watch comes with one year of hardware repair coverage through its limited warranty and up to 90 days of complimentary technical support. Get AppleCare+ to extend your coverage and reduce the stress and cost of unexpected repairs.

reviews.title: Comfortable, worth the value
reviews.text: The band that originally came with the Fitbit was so uncomfortable. It dig into my wrist and left marks. I ordered another band that was about the same price as all four of these. It was useless. Barely fit, and it wouldn’t keep its position, so I decided to try these. First, I couldn’t believe that the same price got me FOUR bands in different colors! The bands are easily adjustable. Once fitted, these stay in place on the wrist. They’re comfy, and they stretch enough so that I can pull off the Fitbit to charge without having to readjust the band. Though inexpensive, this particular purchase was one of the best I’ve had this year, LOL.

reviews.title: Did NOT Work with Sense 2
reviews.text: Not sure what the issue is, but I could not get any of these bands to engage and lock with the Sense 2.  The tab fits into the slot just fine, but it won't engage the locking tab to hold the band into place.  Pulling on the band at all causes it to simply fall out of the watch case.  The bands themselves look fine, but not locking into place obviously makes them worthless for their intended purpose.

reviews.title: Perfect replacement
reviews.text: Exact replacement and a fraction of the price you would pay if you bought it from the store

reviews.title: Blue is more purple
reviews.text: The blue is more purple than it is blue. The color was mostly true to its display photo though, but just a word of caution in case if anyone really wanted a true blue. As for the band itself, I can’t say much about durability since I just installed it. This band comes with 2 securing bands whereas the original Garmin comes with 1. I also feel like the band doesn’t meet flush with the watch face as well as the original Garmin but it’s not bad. This band is also a little stiffer than the floppy Garmin band, and I think I actually like the feel of this stiffer band.

reviews.title: Beautiful Smart Watch
reviews.text: Very pleased.  Handsome titanium case.  Looks more like a  traditional watch than a smart watch.  All functions work on my IPhone 13.  Great choice of faces, including several analog choices.  Always on display is great,  Only one semi-negative.  You must have patience during the pairing process.  Same thing true with programming.  But once it is done, could not ask more from a reasonably priced smart watch. No help from a basically useless manual.  You are on your own.  But, with patience, worth the time it takes to figure out how to pair and operate  Took me about an hour.  Highly recommended

reviews.title: Battery life runs out quickly.
reviews.text: Nice looking.  Great apps and tracking. But battery life isn't all that great.

reviews.title: A good start but needs a lot of work.
reviews.text:
This is a tough review to write. If I could do half stars, this watch would be a 3.5 for me. It does so much right but it feels incomplete and overpriced to me.

First off, the screen and overall build quality is amazing. I have a Galaxy Active 2 as well and, candidly, this Zepp watch is better in pretty much every way in terms of build. The glass feels better and more scratch resistant. You get a stainless frame instead of aluminum. The shocker is the screen. I am not sure how but this watch looks brighter and crisper to me. The size is really nice as well. The Zepp E has a lower profile and almost no visible bezel. If hardware was the lone consideration, this would get a near perfect score.

The battery life is also really outstanding. I get 5 to 7 days with this. It has a proprietary magnetic charger that works well. Even playing with it often and tracking regular workouts doesn't seem to bother it.

I also like Zepp's approach to overall health measurement. The PAI (Personal Activity Intelligence) score tries to give you an overall measurement of how healthy your days are. Once you understand it, I think it is a good general snap shot. If everyone tracked their life like this, the world would be a much healthier place. Sleep tracking is good as well. In line with what I have gotten from Fitbit and Samsung though you need to make sure to set a daily screen timer as it doesn't automatically know to turn off the screen when you are sleeping like the others.

From here though things begin to slip. At $250, there is no onboard GPS. This is a big omission at this price. There also isn't auto tracking for workouts like most others have so you have to remember to physically start tracking workouts. There is no onboard music player though you can control your music from the watch. Interacting with your notifications and inbound calls are all but worthless. Zepp's app is also a mess. It is cumbersome and not well thought out. Everything you want is there but it often takes alot of effort to get from one thing to another. Samsung's app is vastly superior. Lastly, the watch comes with a leather strap. At the time of this writing, Zepp has silicone straps listed on their site but they don't appear to be available yet. A watch that is focused on health should come default with a silicone strap.

I guess I am just not sure what Zepp set out to accomplish with this watch. Is it a smart watch? Not really. Is it a fitness watch? Kind of. Its like they tried to split the baby and ended up accomplishing neither successfully. If you want a good looking everyday digital semi-smart watch with a great battery life, this is worth considering but, for most, at $250, there are better options out there. Perhaps Zepp will update this product and make it more well rounded. If not, I would suggest waiting for a price reduction before investing.

reviews.title: Only thing missing is the talk and messaging
reviews.text: Otherwise, it is similar to Apple watch. I easily set this up and downloaded the software from the app store. It installed easily with absolutely zero issues and you know it is always the software that has glitches. This watch is probably one of the better health smart watches on the market.. It looks nice, it performs nice and has lots of features. You will love the battery life, especially if you have a smartwatch that has to be charged almost daily or maybe ever day and a half. The watch monitors sleep stages, heart rate and 11 different exercises, including swimming. And, it is nice looking. Pretty sweet. I wanted to add that you can select so many faces for your watch without extra charges.

reviews.title: Broke 3 weeks after purchasing
reviews.text: To be fair with the manufacturer, one does not expect for your watch to fall often. In this case, the only time it fell, BOOM - the screen broke on one corner and the display became unusable.

Can be worn while jogging in the rain or doing the dishes) and soft TPE straps which suit wrist sizes from 5.4 to 7.6 inches!

[Convenient USB Charging with Long-Lasting Battery] Enjoy 5-7 days of battery life with 1-2 hours charging time via built-in USB! (During actual use, battery life is related to various factors such as settings, operations, and operating conditions, and therefore may differ from the test conditions)
[A Sweet Life Companion] Never miss your calls, texts, and social app notifications with Octandra Active Exercise Watch!
[More Practical Tools] You can use our running watch as a stop watch, sedentary reminder, alarm watch, and camera shutter remote control of your smartphone!

[Important] This fitness band is not a medical device. All the measurement data and results are provided for reference only and cannot be compared with the results of professional medical devices, used to perform professional diagnosis or monitoring of any medical conditions. Due to external factors (such as arm hair, tattoos, or darker skin), there may be cases where measurement results are inaccurate or even unavailable!

reviews.title: Not a good buy
reviews.text: The 1st one I got "went crazy" and started just cycling thru the various screens on the watch.  I returned it and got a replacement because we all know that "stuff happens".  I got the replacement and it seemed to be working well until day 28, when it stopped connecting with the app at all, even though I installed and uninstalled it several times as the troubleshooting recommended.  Then it started draining my battery dry and that meant it needed to be returned.  Don't waste your money on this one unless you have LOTS of time to fiddle with it!

reviews.title: I wanted to like this product...
reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.
